title: The Swan.

title: Hymn written for the Rev. Mr. Walker, of Chichester.
content:
Where may zeal due succour find,
    Man, for thy unguarded mind?
    To shield thee, when temptations reign,
    From folly's snare, and vice'

title: Two Hymns Written for the Asylum of Female Orphans.
content:
I.

    Parent to those, whose infant days
    No human parent know;
    To thee, O Charity! the praise
    Of filial love shall flow.

title: The Elephant.
content:
Say, nature, on whose wond'rous reign
        Delighted fancy dwells,
     Of all thy numerous brutal train
        What animal excells?

title: The Fatal Horse.
content:
Of creatures that to man attend,
        His pastime, or his wealth;
     The Horse we cherish as a friend,
        To sickness and to health.

title: The Grateful Snake.
content:
Ingratitude! of earth the shame!
     Thou monster, at whose hated name,
        The nerves of kindness ake;
     Would I could drive thee fro

title: Hymn to Contrition.
content:
Tenderest Herald of the sky,
    Nature's safeguard from perdition,
    Friend of sweet, tho' tearful eye,
    Call'd by angels meek Contrition

title: The Panther.
content:

title: The Serpents.
content:
Now blest be Providence divine,
        Surpassing human skill!
     That often takes from things malign,
        The privilege of ill.

content:
Dear nymph of a feeling, and delicate mind!
    Whose eye the rash tears of timidity blind,

title: Hymn for Christmas Day.
content:
Saviour inspire the voice of earth,
    To hail the day that gave Thee birth;
    The Heavens resound in blest accord,

title: The Goat.
content:
"Can mothers of our English isle,
        The pride of all the earth,
     From any tribe of tender brutes,
        A mother's duly learn?"

title: A Morning Hymn.
content:
Awake my soul! in cheerful mood,
    Thy matin thanks to pay!
    The God, who gives thee rest, and food,
    Directs thee to be gay.

    The

title: Hymn to the Creator.
content:
Source of all kind, all potent thought!
    Thou God of Goodness, and of Power!
    In Thee my soul, by trouble taught,
    Shall trust, as in

title: The Eagle.
content:
Nature, what heart may here by thee,
        Most truly brave be styled?
     The tender mother's it must be,
        When struggling for her

title: The Dog.
content:
Of all the speechless friends of man
        The faithful dog I deem
     Deserving from the human clan
        The tenderest esteem:

     T

title: The Baya: Or The Indian Bird.
content:
Let the Nightingale still be renown'd for her song,
        The Eagle for strength, and for softness the Dove,
     Higher praise to the Baya o

title: Hymn.
content:
Since the Evening of Life will soon close,
    While I live, may I justly incline
    To diffuse peace of heart among those,
    Whose lives ma

title: Hymn on Charity.
content:
Nor faith, nor hope, whate'er their force,
    Can aught avail the soul,
    Should charity not guide its course
    To glory's heavenly goal.

title: Felpham: An Epistle To Henrietta Of Lavant.

content:

title: Hymn to the Saviour.
content:
Saviour! pure source of life and zeal intense,
    Whose words were peace, whose deeds benificence,
    Around thy servant ever may I see
    T

title: The Halcyon.
content:
Not only men of stormy minds,
        The storms of trouble know,
     All creatures of this earth must find
        A share of earthly woe!

title: The Hermit's Dog.
content:
Of dogs who sav'd a living friend,
        Most nobly, ye have read:
     Now to a nobler still attend,
        A guardian of the dead.

title: On The Fear Of Death: An Epistle To A Lady.

title: Hymn to Humility
content:
Of all the Christian virtues chief
    With modest charms, and mild relief,
    Most apt to heal the wounds of pride, and spleen,

title: The Lion.
content:
